channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Syrian dictator Bashar Assad flees into exile as Islamist rebels conquer country
item.description: A lightening Syrian Islamist offensive over just days contributed to the demise of the over 50-year reign of the Assad Baathist regime in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian insurgents reach gates of Damascus, threatening decades-long Assad regime
item.description: Syrian insurgents have reached the gates of the nation's capital, shortly after Bashar al-Assad's government abandoned the key city of Homs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's president avoids an impeachment attempt over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol avoided an opposition-led attempt to impeach him over his short-lived imposition of martial law, as most ruling party lawmakers boycotted a parliamentary vote Saturday to deny a two-thirds majority needed to suspend his presid
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Assad regime in peril as insurgents near Syria's capital: What to know
item.description: Syrian rebels on Saturday battled government forces in an effort to take control of the crucial city of Homs as they advanced toward the country's capital, Damascus -- moves that catapulted President Bashar al-Assad's nearly 30 years of power into peril, Reuters reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Trump, Jill Biden attend Notre Dame reopening
item.description: President-elect Trump and first lady Jill Biden attended the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris, sitting in the same row during the ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Trump meets with Macron, Zelensky in France
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump visited with French President Emmanuel Macron on Saturday in Paris, for a meeting that he said was to discuss the world going "crazy.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Pope creates 21 cardinals, many of them reformers in their own right, to carry out his reform plans
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis on Saturday installed 21 new cardinals, many of whom are key figures in his reform agenda: A Dominican preacher who acted as the spiritual father for Francis' recent gathering of bishops, a Neapolitan “street priest” like himself, and a Peruvian bishop who has strongly backe
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An explosion destroys an apartment block in a Dutch city, killing at least 3 and injuring others
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An explosion and fire rocked a neighborhood in the Dutch city of The Hague on Saturday, killing three people and injuring other people and destroying several apartments, according to authorities. The cause of the disaster was unclear.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Americans pressed to leave Syria amid ‘volatile and unpredictable’ clashes
item.description: The U.S. the Embassy in Syria issued an urgent warning on Saturday encouraging citizens to evacuate amid escalating conflict in the region.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire five years ago nearly destroyed the beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Storm Darragh batters UK and Ireland, leaving 1 dead and hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A man died and hundreds of thousands of people in Britain and Ireland were left without power Saturday as high winds and heavy rain battered the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Syria's forces withdraw from Homs, a key link between the capital and coast
item.description: Syrian opposition fighters have reached the suburbs of the capital, Damascus, and government forces abandoned the central city of Homs as the rebels’ surprising offensive picks up speed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against 'Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: As part of an investigation into the ‘Ndrangheta mafia in the city of Brescia, Italian police have arrested 25 people including a nun known for her prison work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Romania's top court annuls first round of presidential vote won by far-right candidate
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A top Romanian court on Friday annulled the first round of the country's presidential election, days after allegations emerged that Russia ran a coordinated online campaign to promote the far-right outsider who won the first round.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will decide if Palestinian authorities can be sued in US over attacks in Middle East
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed Friday to settle a years-long legal dispute over whether Palestinian authorities can be sued in U.S. courts by Americans killed or wounded in terrorism attacks in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to consider reviving terror victims’ suits against Palestinian leadership
item.description: The Supreme Court said Friday it will consider upholding a law that allows Americans injured by acts of terror to take Palestinian leadership groups to U.S. courts for damages.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Turkey’s Erdogan encourages Islamist rebels to continue advances as Assad regime scrambles to survive
item.description: Amid a fast moving seizure of Syrian cities by a U.S.-classified Islamist terrorist movement, the power politics of the Mideast is rapidly changing quickly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran launches rocket with heaviest-ever payload into space amid heightened concern over nuclear program
item.description: Iran launches rocket into space carrying its heaviest-ever payload, as security experts remain concerned by its "drastically" increased stockpiles of enriched uranium near nuclear weapons grade purity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Romanian court throws out presidential results after Russian interference allegations
item.description: Romania’s top court on Friday wiped out results from the first round of presidential elections and called for a rerun, days after declassified Romanian security documents alleged Russian interference that promoted a far-right outsider candidate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Berlin's new panda twins have been named. Meet Leni and Lotti, or Meng Hao and Meng Tian
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's new panda twins now have names — or rather, two names apiece. The two female cubs, born at the Berlin Zoo on Aug. 22, were introduced Friday as Meng Hao and Meng Tian ("beautiful dreams" and “sweet dreams”).
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ailing kids wait months for Israeli permission to leave Gaza for treatment. Some die in the meantime
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The 12-year-old Palestinian boy was lying in a hospital bed in central Gaza, wracked with leukemia, malnourished and whimpering in pain despite the morphine doctors were giving him, when Rosalia Bollen, a UNICEF official, said she saw him in late October.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Metal detectorist uncovers fragment of Viking sword in the Netherlands
item.description: A 10th-century Viking sword fragment was discovered by a metal detectorist scouring a field in the Netherlands. Further research is being done to find out more about the ancient item.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump heads to France as world leaders seek to curry favor
item.description: President-elect Trump is holding court with foreign leaders as they seek to gain influence and insight into his mindset before he takes office, with the expectation that the Trump administration will bring major shifts to the global economy and to its approach to alliances.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Canada bans more assault firearms, suggests donating guns to Ukraine
item.description: Top leaders in Canada announced that they had banned an additional 324 assault-style firearms in a continued effort for more stringent gun control.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actress dies after consuming amphibian's venom at spiritual cleansing retreat
item.description: Mexican actress Marcela Alcázar Rodríguez, 33, died after ingesting Amazonian frog venom during a cleansing ritual at a spiritual retreat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Assad regime under major pressure as Islamist rebels seize more territory; Israel, US watch
item.description: The conflict in Syria has escalated dramatically, and Islamist rebels have caught many by surprise with rapid territorial gains at the expense of the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Masked man arrested outside Swedish royal family residence with knife, handcuffs, tape: reports
item.description: A man acting suspiciously while armed outside the home of a Swedish royal family was arrested Wednesday evening, authorities told media outlets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Qatar returns to Hamas-Israel negotiations as Trump envoy looks to make inroads
item.description: Qatar has once again resumed its top role in helping negotiate a cease-fire between Israel and Hamas to return hostages in Gaza, as President-elect Donald Trump's Middle East envoy begins meeting with top leaders amid crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kosovo accuses Serbia of 'terrorist attack' resembling Russian actions in Ukraine
item.description: Kosovo accused Serbia of carrying out a terrorist attack on a water pipeline in the Serb majority north of Kosovo. Serbia denies the accusations, but the recent bloodshed highlights that peace in the Balkans is far from certain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president to face impeachment vote this weekend over martial law order, lawmakers say
item.description: South Korean opposition lawmakers will hold a vote to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol after the president declared martial law on Tuesday, citing "anti-state" activities by his opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli strikes on a Gaza tent camp kill at least 21 people, hospital says
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes tore through a tent camp for displaced Palestinians in southern Gaza on Wednesday, sparking fires and killing at least 21 people, according to the head of a nearby hospital, in the latest assault on a sprawling tent city that Israel designated a huma
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago welcomes eight children from Gaza for medical treatment
item.description: Over the next few months, the children will receive medical treatment at U.S. hospitals, free of cost.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean parliament votes to defy president by lifting his declaration of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law late Tuesday, vowing to eliminate “anti-state” forces as he struggles against an opposition that controls the country’s parliament and that he accuses of sympathizing with communist North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9
